details: The npm package dependency_confusions version 99.9.9 performs install-time host fingerprint and project path exfiltration. package.json sets index.js as the package main entrypoint and defines postinstall: node index.js. During npm install, index.js builds a JSON payload containing the local username from os.userInfo().username, the current project path from INIT_CWD or process.cwd(), the hostname from os.hostname(), the first non-internal local IPv4 address from os.networkInterfaces(), the platform from os.platform(), and an install timestamp. It then sends that payload with https.request to a webhook.site endpoint. This leaks developer or CI host identity and project path context to an attacker-controlled endpoint without user consent and is typical dependency-confusion reconnaissance behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code

cwes.description: The product exposes sensitive information to an actor that is not explicitly authorized to have access to that information.
cwes.name: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
